model_summary: Initial gVAR-compatible observation model to operationalize 6 criteria (C01–C06) describing muscle tension, interoceptive awareness, tension-linked headaches, anticipatory threat, relaxation capacity, and habitual bracing, plus 5 modifiable predictors (P01–P05) targeting behavioral, cognitive, and sleep/ Social domains. Designed for daily EMAs and brief diaries with consistent sampling_per_day to enable within-person gVAR analyses. Dense P→C, P→P, and C→C relation grids are provided to support data-driven predictor selection and subsequent causal-network estimation.
variable_selection_notes: C01–C06 cover physiological arousal, somatic awareness, headache linkage, cognitive threat processing, relaxation, and habitual postural bracing. Predictors (P01–P05) emphasize: PMR adherence, interoception training, sleep optimization, social support, and mindfulness practice. All variables are designed for short, periodic self-report entries (1–9 Likert scales) to maximize ecological validity and reduce respondent burden.

momentary_ema.notes: Prompts distributed across daytime blocks to capture within-day fluctuations in tension and anticipatory states.

daily_diary.notes: End-of-day reflections on relaxation and threat appraisal.

passive_streams.notes: No passive sensing in initial design.

design_recommendations.gvar_readiness_notes: 11-node configuration is feasible; plan for moderate missingness and simple imputation.
design_recommendations.variance_support_notes: Expect moderate within-person variance; include diurnal controls where feasible.
design_recommendations.missingness_risk_notes: Prompt reminders and brief check-ins to mitigate missingness.

safety_notes: This is not a clinical diagnosis. If acute risk is suspected, seek immediate professional help.

criteria_variables.label: Elevated resting muscle tension in shoulders and jaw

measurement.notes: Self-reported intensity of shoulder/jaw tension; corroborate with headaches.

criteria_variables.mapped_leaf_full_path: CLINICAL / ICD-10 / pain disorders / orofacial pain disorders / temporomandibular joint disorders / neck_muscle_tension_associated_with_tmjd
criteria_variables.mapped_leaf_embed_path: CLINICAL / ICD-10 / pain disorders / orofacial pain disorders / temporomandibular joint disorders / *neck_muscle_tension_associated_with_tmjd
criteria_variables.mapped_rationale: Variable C01 reports elevated resting muscle tension in shoulders and jaw. Among candidate CRITERION leaves, neck_muscle_tension_associated_with_tmjd specifically encodes neck/shoulder muscle tension in the context of temporomandibular joint disorders, which directly matches the anatomy (shoulders and jaw) and the mechanism (muscle tension contributing to somatic arousal and headache risk). The EMA_self_report measurement (since_last_prompt, 0-1 scale) can quantify the intensity of this tension in real time. Therefore this leaf is the most specific operational construct aligning with C01's intention.
criteria_variables.mapping_query_text_used: Elevated resting muscle tension in shoulders and jaw | Operational item/signal: criterion_presence_intensity | Polarity: higher_is_worse | Notes: Self-reported intensity of shoulder/jaw tension; corroborate with headaches.

criteria_variables.label: Low moment-to-moment awareness of bodily tension

measurement.notes: Higher scores indicate greater unawareness of tension.

criteria_variables.mapped_leaf_full_path: NON-CLINICAL / AGE-GENERAL / physical / physical self-care / listening to body signals / symptom awareness / awareness of physical changes
criteria_variables.mapped_leaf_embed_path: NON-CLINICAL / AGE-GENERAL / physical / physical self-care / listening to body signals / symptom awareness / *awareness of physical changes
criteria_variables.mapped_rationale: C02 targets interoceptive (bodily) awareness, i.e., awareness of internal bodily signals such as muscle tension. The measurement item is interoceptive_awareness_level via EMA, 1–9 scale, two prompts per day, focused on reporting awareness of bodily tension. Among the candidates, 3901 (awareness of physical changes) is the closest operational leaf for interoceptive signal awareness in a general (non-age-specific) adult context, capturing the core construct of noticing physical cues and bodily changes, which aligns with low moment-to-moment awareness of tension. While not a perfect one-to-one label, it best matches the intended interoceptive awareness domain given the available leaf taxonomy.
criteria_variables.mapping_query_text_used: Low moment-to-moment awareness of bodily tension | Operational item/signal: interoceptive_awareness_level | Polarity: higher_is_worse | Notes: Higher scores indicate greater unawareness of tension.

criteria_variables.mapped_rationale: Best match: C03 describes headaches triggered by muscular tension; tension-type headaches are characterized by a pressing/tightening quality and non-pulsating pain that is often bilateral. The candidate leaf 16167 corresponds to tension-type headache with pressing_tightening_quality, the most specific fit among the provided leaves for a muscular-tension-triggered headache in an EMA self-report context.

criteria_variables.label: Persistent anticipatory threat expectation

measurement.notes: Subjective expectation of threat across contexts.

criteria_variables.mapped_leaf_embed_path: CLINICAL / DSM-5TR / personality disorders / cluster a (odd, eccentric) / paranoid personality disorder / *hypervigilance_to_potential_threats
criteria_variables.mapped_rationale: Selected leaf maps best to 'Persistent anticipatory threat expectation' by anchoring on hypervigilance to potential threats as the core mechanism underlying sustained anticipation of threat. The variable is EMA-based, momentary assessment of anticipatory_threat_experience (1–9 scale, two prompts/day), which aligns with monitoring/threat anticipation patterns captured by 'hypervigilance_to_potential_threats' leaf. Although the leaf is labeled under paranoid personality disorder, it reflects a general threat-sensitivity construct relevant to C04 in this pseudoprofile (interoceptive/somatic arousal and cognitive threat processing). This leaf is the most specific match among candidates; others are more domain- or context-specific (e.g., performance-based anticipatory anxiety, phobia-specific), or less directly about threat anticipation than vigilance.
criteria_variables.mapping_query_text_used: Persistent anticipatory threat expectation | Operational item/signal: anticipatory_threat_experience | Polarity: higher_is_worse | Notes: Subjective expectation of threat across contexts.

criteria_variables.label: Difficulty relaxing on low-stress days

measurement.notes: Perceived ability to downregulate arousal in calm contexts.

criteria_variables.mapped_leaf_full_path: NON-CLINICAL / AGE-GENERAL / emotional / positive emotions / contentment / relaxation / mental relaxation
criteria_variables.mapped_leaf_embed_path: NON-CLINICAL / AGE-GENERAL / emotional / positive emotions / contentment / relaxation / *mental relaxation
criteria_variables.mapped_rationale: Variable C05 (Difficulty relaxing on low-stress days) targets the internal relaxation capacity in calm contexts. Among candidates, idx 5617 corresponds to 'mental relaxation' under emotional/positive-emotions contentment/relaxation, aligning with an internal cognitive-affective state of relaxing (downregulating arousal) on low-stress days. The measurement item 'relaxation_capacity_in_chill' assessed via EMA_self_report (1–9 scale, momentary) fits this latent construct better than external relaxation techniques or stress-reduction strategies. This leaf provides the most specific operational match to the intended variable within the pseudoprofile context of daily EMA assessments.
criteria_variables.mapping_query_text_used: Difficulty relaxing on low-stress days | Operational item/signal: relaxation_capacity_in_chill | Polarity: higher_is_worse | Notes: Perceived ability to downregulate arousal in calm contexts.

criteria_variables.mapped_rationale: C06 describes habitual anticipatory muscle bracing (muscle tension) in the absence of immediate threat. The EMA self-report of muscle_bracing_frequency targets the same mechanism (anticipatory somatic tension). Among candidates, the closest leaf is 'headache_or_muscle_tension_due_to_anxiety' (CLINICAL / DSM-5TR / bipolar and related disorders / specifiers / with anxious distress / headache_or_muscle_tension_due_to_anxiety), which explicitly links muscle tension to anxiety/anticipatory states. This provides the most specific operational counterpart for anticipatory, non-immediate-threat–driven muscle bracing within the available leaves.

predictor_variables.label: Daily progressive muscle relaxation (PMR) adherence
predictor_variables.mechanism_rationale: Directly reduces muscular arousal and somatic tension.
predictor_variables.mapped_leaf_full_path: PSYCHO / core_psychological_skills_toolkits / stress_resilience_and_downregulation / physiological_downshift / breath_and_relaxation_skills / progressive_muscle_relaxation_practice
predictor_variables.mapped_rationale: Best-matching predictor leaf: progressive_muscle_relaxation_practice (PSYCHO / core_psychological_skills_toolkits / stress_resilience_and_downregulation / physiological_downshift / breath_and_relaxation_skills / progressive_muscle_relaxation_practice). It directly aligns with the variable label Daily PMR adherence and with the measurement item pmr_adherence (EMA self-report). Mechanistically, PMR practice reduces muscular arousal and somatic tension, addressing the physical arousal and relaxation capacity aspects described (C01–C06). The pseudoprofile context emphasizes daily brief EMAs, so a PMR-practice construct fits as the most specific operational predictor for adherence to PMR within the PSYCHO domain.
predictor_variables.mapping_query_text_used: Daily progressive muscle relaxation (PMR) adherence | Domain: PSYCHO | Mechanism: Directly reduces muscular arousal and somatic tension. | Intervention examples: PMR practice sessions; safe breathing. | Measurement: EMA_self_report | pmr_adherence | Prior ontology hint: PSYCHO / core_psychological_skills_toolkits / stress_resilience_and_downregulation / physiological_downshift / breath_and_relaxation_skills

predictor_variables.label: Interoceptive awareness training uptake
predictor_variables.mechanism_rationale: Improves detection of bodily cues, enabling timely regulation.
predictor_variables.mapped_rationale: Best-match leaf is PSYCHO / integrative_creative_and_body_oriented_interventions / somatic_and_body_oriented_psychotherapies / interoceptive_awareness_training (idx 2964). It directly corresponds to interoceptive_awareness_training as a training uptake within the PSYCHO domain; the variable P02 is measured as momentary self-report of uptake (EMA), aligning with the leaf's focus on engagement with interoceptive training; mechanism aligns with improved detection of bodily cues enabling regulation. Other mindfulness/body-sensation leaves cover related practices but are less specific to uptake of interoceptive training.
predictor_variables.mapping_query_text_used: Interoceptive awareness training uptake | Domain: PSYCHO | Mechanism: Improves detection of bodily cues, enabling timely regulation. | Intervention examples: Brief mindfulness body-scan; safe participation. | Measurement: EMA_self_report | interoception_training_uptake | Prior ontology hint: PSYCHO / mindfulness_and_bodily_sensations / interoception_training

predictor_variables.label: Sleep optimization adherence and circadian alignment
predictor_variables.mechanism_rationale: Aligns arousal regulation with restorative sleep.
predictor_variables.intervention_examples_safe: Sleep schedule regularization; light exposure strategies.
predictor_variables.mapped_rationale: Variable maps to Sleep optimization adherence and circadian alignment with measurement sleep_routine_adherence (end_of_day EMA). The best-match leaf is BIO / medical_assessment_and_physiology_testing / sleep_and_respiratory_testing / circadian_and_sleep_tracking / sleep_diary, which directly captures daily sleep routines and circadian-related sleep tracking, aligning with the intended end-of-day self-report of sleep routine adherence. This is more specific than other circadian_entrainment leaves (e.g., meal timing or light cues) and directly represents a daily diary of sleep patterns.
predictor_variables.mapping_query_text_used: Sleep optimization adherence and circadian alignment | Domain: BIO | Mechanism: Aligns arousal regulation with restorative sleep. | Intervention examples: Sleep schedule regularization; light exposure strategies. | Measurement: EMA_self_report | sleep_routine_adherence | Prior ontology hint: BIO / Sleep_Circadian_and_Restoration / Circadian_Entrainment

predictor_variables.label: Social support engagement
predictor_variables.mapped_rationale: Variable: P04 Social support engagement. EMA_self_report with measurement item 'social_support_engagement' aligns with a SOCIAL leaf that explicitly encodes engagement within a social/support network context. Among candidates, idx 629 uniquely contains the engagement concept in its path ('healthy_online_community_engagement' and 'moderated_support_community_enrollment'), matching the intended measurement mode (EMA in mobile context) and the mechanism of social support engagement as a buffer for stress/arousal. This is the most specific operational construct for social support engagement within the provided leaves, matching the label, measurement item, and domain.
predictor_variables.mapping_query_text_used: Social support engagement | Domain: SOCIAL | Mechanism: Buffers stress; reduces overall arousal. | Intervention examples: Weekly check-in with supportive person. | Measurement: EMA_self_report | social_support_engagement | Prior ontology hint: SOCIAL / social_support_and_coherence / engagement

predictor_variables.label: Mindfulness/Attention regulation practice
predictor_variables.mechanism_rationale: Enhances attentional control and reduces threat reactivity.
predictor_variables.intervention_examples_safe: Brief body-scan; focus on present moment.
predictor_variables.mapped_rationale: Selected leaf idx 3586 (body_scan_practice). This is the most specific operational construct matching 'Mindfulness/Attention regulation practice' among the candidates, aligning with the variable's intervention_examples_safe (Brief body-scan) and measurement item mindfulness_practice_engagement (EMA self-report). The leaf sits within mindfulness_based_interventions / body_based_mindfulness and represents a concrete body-based mindfulness practice aimed at attentional regulation, fitting the mechanism rationale 'Enhances attentional control and reduces threat reactivity.' It avoids overfitting to unique subtypes and remains consistent with pseudoprofile context as a modifiable predictor (P05).
predictor_variables.mapping_query_text_used: Mindfulness/Attention regulation practice | Domain: PSYCHO | Mechanism: Enhances attentional control and reduces threat reactivity. | Intervention examples: Brief body-scan; focus on present moment. | Measurement: EMA_self_report | mindfulness_practice_engagement | Prior ontology hint: PSYCHO / third_wave_and_contextual_therapies / mindfulness_based_interventions / body_based_mindfulness

edges.notes: PMR adherence inversely related to shoulder/jaw tension.
Higher PMR adherence reduces tension awareness.
PMR may reduce headaches via tension reduction.
PMR reduces anticipatory threat via arousal downshift.
Interoceptive training improves detection of tension.
Increase in awareness may reduce bracing.

source_variable.label: Elevated resting muscle tension in shoulders and jaw

measurement.notes: Self-reported intensity of shoulder/jaw tension; corroborate with headaches.

criteria.label: Low moment-to-moment awareness of bodily tension
criteria.query_text_used: Low moment-to-moment awareness of bodily tension | Operational item/signal: interoceptive_awareness_level | Polarity: higher_is_worse | Notes: Higher scores indicate greater unawareness of tension.

mapping.rationale: C02 targets interoceptive (bodily) awareness, i.e., awareness of internal bodily signals such as muscle tension. The measurement item is interoceptive_awareness_level via EMA, 1–9 scale, two prompts per day, focused on reporting awareness of bodily tension. Among the candidates, 3901 (awareness of physical changes) is the closest operational leaf for interoceptive signal awareness in a general (non-age-specific) adult context, capturing the core construct of noticing physical cues and bodily changes, which aligns with low moment-to-moment awareness of tension. While not a perfect one-to-one label, it best matches the intended interoceptive awareness domain given the available leaf taxonomy.

source_variable.label: Low moment-to-moment awareness of bodily tension

measurement.notes: Higher scores indicate greater unawareness of tension.

criteria.label: Headaches triggered by muscular tension
criteria.query_text_used: Headaches triggered by muscular tension | Operational item/signal: headache_presence_and_tension_link | Polarity: higher_is_worse | Notes: Record headaches and alignment with tension episodes.

mapping.rationale: Best match: C03 describes headaches triggered by muscular tension; tension-type headaches are characterized by a pressing/tightening quality and non-pulsating pain that is often bilateral. The candidate leaf 16167 corresponds to tension-type headache with pressing_tightening_quality, the most specific fit among the provided leaves for a muscular-tension-triggered headache in an EMA self-report context.

criteria.label: Persistent anticipatory threat expectation
criteria.query_text_used: Persistent anticipatory threat expectation | Operational item/signal: anticipatory_threat_experience | Polarity: higher_is_worse | Notes: Subjective expectation of threat across contexts.

mapping.rationale: Selected leaf maps best to 'Persistent anticipatory threat expectation' by anchoring on hypervigilance to potential threats as the core mechanism underlying sustained anticipation of threat. The variable is EMA-based, momentary assessment of anticipatory_threat_experience (1–9 scale, two prompts/day), which aligns with monitoring/threat anticipation patterns captured by 'hypervigilance_to_potential_threats' leaf. Although the leaf is labeled under paranoid personality disorder, it reflects a general threat-sensitivity construct relevant to C04 in this pseudoprofile (interoceptive/somatic arousal and cognitive threat processing). This leaf is the most specific match among candidates; others are more domain- or context-specific (e.g., performance-based anticipatory anxiety, phobia-specific), or less directly about threat anticipation than vigilance.

criteria.label: Difficulty relaxing on low-stress days
criteria.query_text_used: Difficulty relaxing on low-stress days | Operational item/signal: relaxation_capacity_in_chill | Polarity: higher_is_worse | Notes: Perceived ability to downregulate arousal in calm contexts.

mapping.rationale: Variable C05 (Difficulty relaxing on low-stress days) targets the internal relaxation capacity in calm contexts. Among candidates, idx 5617 corresponds to 'mental relaxation' under emotional/positive-emotions contentment/relaxation, aligning with an internal cognitive-affective state of relaxing (downregulating arousal) on low-stress days. The measurement item 'relaxation_capacity_in_chill' assessed via EMA_self_report (1–9 scale, momentary) fits this latent construct better than external relaxation techniques or stress-reduction strategies. This leaf provides the most specific operational match to the intended variable within the pseudoprofile context of daily EMA assessments.

criteria.label: Habitual anticipatory muscle bracing in absence of immediate threat
criteria.query_text_used: Habitual anticipatory muscle bracing in absence of immediate threat | Operational item/signal: muscle_bracing_frequency | Polarity: higher_is_worse | Notes: Capture habitual bracing via prompts.

mapping.rationale: C06 describes habitual anticipatory muscle bracing (muscle tension) in the absence of immediate threat. The EMA self-report of muscle_bracing_frequency targets the same mechanism (anticipatory somatic tension). Among candidates, the closest leaf is 'headache_or_muscle_tension_due_to_anxiety' (CLINICAL / DSM-5TR / bipolar and related disorders / specifiers / with anxious distress / headache_or_muscle_tension_due_to_anxiety), which explicitly links muscle tension to anxiety/anticipatory states. This provides the most specific operational counterpart for anticipatory, non-immediate-threat–driven muscle bracing within the available leaves.

source_variable.label: Habitual anticipatory muscle bracing in absence of immediate threat

predictors.label: Daily progressive muscle relaxation (PMR) adherence
predictors.query_text_used: Daily progressive muscle relaxation (PMR) adherence | Domain: PSYCHO | Mechanism: Directly reduces muscular arousal and somatic tension. | Intervention examples: PMR practice sessions; safe breathing. | Measurement: EMA_self_report | pmr_adherence | Prior ontology hint: PSYCHO / core_psychological_skills_toolkits / stress_resilience_and_downregulation / physiological_downshift / breath_and_relaxation_skills

mapping.rationale: Best-matching predictor leaf: progressive_muscle_relaxation_practice (PSYCHO / core_psychological_skills_toolkits / stress_resilience_and_downregulation / physiological_downshift / breath_and_relaxation_skills / progressive_muscle_relaxation_practice). It directly aligns with the variable label Daily PMR adherence and with the measurement item pmr_adherence (EMA self-report). Mechanistically, PMR practice reduces muscular arousal and somatic tension, addressing the physical arousal and relaxation capacity aspects described (C01–C06). The pseudoprofile context emphasizes daily brief EMAs, so a PMR-practice construct fits as the most specific operational predictor for adherence to PMR within the PSYCHO domain.

predictors.label: Interoceptive awareness training uptake
predictors.query_text_used: Interoceptive awareness training uptake | Domain: PSYCHO | Mechanism: Improves detection of bodily cues, enabling timely regulation. | Intervention examples: Brief mindfulness body-scan; safe participation. | Measurement: EMA_self_report | interoception_training_uptake | Prior ontology hint: PSYCHO / mindfulness_and_bodily_sensations / interoception_training

mapping.rationale: Best-match leaf is PSYCHO / integrative_creative_and_body_oriented_interventions / somatic_and_body_oriented_psychotherapies / interoceptive_awareness_training (idx 2964). It directly corresponds to interoceptive_awareness_training as a training uptake within the PSYCHO domain; the variable P02 is measured as momentary self-report of uptake (EMA), aligning with the leaf's focus on engagement with interoceptive training; mechanism aligns with improved detection of bodily cues enabling regulation. Other mindfulness/body-sensation leaves cover related practices but are less specific to uptake of interoceptive training.

source_variable.label: Interoceptive awareness training uptake
source_variable.mechanism_rationale: Improves detection of bodily cues, enabling timely regulation.

predictors.label: Sleep optimization adherence and circadian alignment
predictors.query_text_used: Sleep optimization adherence and circadian alignment | Domain: BIO | Mechanism: Aligns arousal regulation with restorative sleep. | Intervention examples: Sleep schedule regularization; light exposure strategies. | Measurement: EMA_self_report | sleep_routine_adherence | Prior ontology hint: BIO / Sleep_Circadian_and_Restoration / Circadian_Entrainment

mapping.rationale: Variable maps to Sleep optimization adherence and circadian alignment with measurement sleep_routine_adherence (end_of_day EMA). The best-match leaf is BIO / medical_assessment_and_physiology_testing / sleep_and_respiratory_testing / circadian_and_sleep_tracking / sleep_diary, which directly captures daily sleep routines and circadian-related sleep tracking, aligning with the intended end-of-day self-report of sleep routine adherence. This is more specific than other circadian_entrainment leaves (e.g., meal timing or light cues) and directly represents a daily diary of sleep patterns.

predictors.label: Mindfulness/Attention regulation practice
predictors.query_text_used: Mindfulness/Attention regulation practice | Domain: PSYCHO | Mechanism: Enhances attentional control and reduces threat reactivity. | Intervention examples: Brief body-scan; focus on present moment. | Measurement: EMA_self_report | mindfulness_practice_engagement | Prior ontology hint: PSYCHO / third_wave_and_contextual_therapies / mindfulness_based_interventions / body_based_mindfulness

mapping.rationale: Selected leaf idx 3586 (body_scan_practice). This is the most specific operational construct matching 'Mindfulness/Attention regulation practice' among the candidates, aligning with the variable's intervention_examples_safe (Brief body-scan) and measurement item mindfulness_practice_engagement (EMA self-report). The leaf sits within mindfulness_based_interventions / body_based_mindfulness and represents a concrete body-based mindfulness practice aimed at attentional regulation, fitting the mechanism rationale 'Enhances attentional control and reduces threat reactivity.' It avoids overfitting to unique subtypes and remains consistent with pseudoprofile context as a modifiable predictor (P05).
